title: Parameter validation in yielding methods should be wrapped
description: Parameter validation in yielding methods should be wrappedAnalyze your codeconsistency-conventionalmaintainabilityCode SmellyieldWhy is this an issue?Because of the wayyieldmethods are rewritten by the compiler (they become lazily evaluated state machines) any exceptions thrown during the parameters check will happen only when the collection is iterated over. That could happen far away from the source of the buggy code.Therefore it is recommended to split the method into two: an outer method handling the validation (no longer lazy) and an inner (lazy) method to handle the iteration.This rule raises an issue when a method throws any exception derived fromArgumentExceptionand contains theyieldkeyword.Noncompliant code examplepublic static IEnumerable<TSource> TakeWhile<TSource>(this IEnumera
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

logic.detection_conditions: Detects methods that contain both argument validation (throwing ArgumentException family) and yield return/break statements within the same method body
logic.triggering_conditions: Method contains ArgumentException throwing AND contains yield return/break statements
logic.exclusion_conditions: Test methods, methods with Assert/Console/Logger/Debug calls, generated code
logic.reasoning: Parameter validation in yielding methods should be separated from the iterator implementation to ensure immediate validation failure rather than deferred exceptions when the collection is enumerated

should_trigger: public IEnumerable<T> TakeWhile<T>(IEnumerable<T> source) { if (source == null) throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(source)); foreach (var item in source) yield return item; }
should_not_trigger: public IEnumerable<T> TakeWhile<T>(IEnumerable<T> source) { if (source == null) throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(source)); return TakeWhileCore(source); }
